description_long: This is a rustic wooden bucket with a cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by two dark metal hoops, one near the top and one near the bottom. The wood has a natural, weathered appearance with visible grain and a gradient of color from light tan at the top to a darker brown near the base, suggesting age or use. A thick, twisted rope serves as a flexible bail handle, attached to the bucket through holes on opposite sides and knotted securely. The interior is smooth and matches the exterior wood, with no visible lining or coating. The bucket is open at the top, with no lid or cover present, and the rope handle is substantial enough for easy carrying. This type of bucket would be suitable for carrying water, sand, or other materials in an urban or garden setting.
description: A weathered wooden bucket with dark metal hoops and a thick, twisted rope handle, open at the top and showing natural wood grain. The bucket has a rustic, utilitarian appearance suitable for outdoor or garden use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open top, full rope handle, and both knots securing the handle to the bucket.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting one side knot of the rope handle and the curvature of the wooden staves.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the continuous rope handle and the even distribution of the metal hoops around the bucket.
description_view_3: Angled view from the opposite side, focusing on the other rope knot and the bucket's round shape.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: metal hoops at top and bottom
